statements: Measure serum CA125 in primary care in women with symptoms suggesting ovarian cancer.
If CA125 is 35 IU/ml or greater, arrange ultrasound of abdomen and pelvis.
If ultrasound suggests ovarian cancer, refer via suspected cancer pathway.
RMI I >=250 should be referred to specialist MDT.
USC referral for raised CA125 >=35 IU/ml.
USC referral for ultrasound suggesting ovarian cancer or abdominal/pelvic mass not clearly uterine/GI/urological.
USC referral for unexplained ascites.
In symptomatic women (especially >=50), do abdominal exam + CA125 + urgent pelvic USS.
Simple unilateral unilocular cyst <5 cm: no follow-up.
Simple 5-7 cm: repeat TVUS every 6 months for 1 year; refer if persistent/enlarging.
>7 cm: refer to gynaecology with CA125.
RMI >200: USOC referral and oncology MDT route.
If age <40 and non-simple mass: add LDH, AFP, hCG.
Simple cyst <3 cm: no follow-up.
Simple 3-5 cm with CA125<35: repeat TVUS + CA125 6-monthly up to 1 year.
Simple >5 cm: urgent referral to gynaecology (USOC if symptomatic).
Any non-simple/indeterminate mass: refer with CA125 and RMI.
RMI >200: USOC pathway + MDT escalation.
Premenopausal simple cyst <5 cm usually physiological; no routine follow-up.
Premenopausal 5-7 cm: interval USS (3-6 monthly).
Premenopausal >7 cm: consider surgical management/referral.
Postmenopausal simple <5 cm + normal CA125: conservative with interval scanning.
RMI >200 predicts increased malignancy risk and supports oncology escalation.
evidence.limitations: Review date older (2020/2023) than GGC 2025-2026 pathways.

statements: Postmenopausal pathway uses CA125 + TVUS + RMI I.
RMI I threshold of 200 commonly used for low vs increased risk pathways.
Dec 2025 update: simple unilateral unilocular postmenopausal cysts <=3 cm do not need routine follow-up.
Many premenopausal simple/functional cysts <50 mm resolve within 2-3 cycles.
Laparoscopy is preferred for benign masses when surgery is needed.
IOTA malignant-rule features should trigger oncology referral.
SIGN provides Scottish national ovarian cancer management framework, supporting specialist pathway escalation for suspected malignancy.
evidence.limitations: PDF text extraction in this run was technically limited; used validated indexed metadata/snippets.

statements: CKS page present but access-limited; no additional extracted threshold statements in this run.
